key_points: The degree of a polynomial decides the maximum number of zeroes it can have
Zeroes of a polynomial are the solutions of the equation p(x) = 0
The graph of a polynomial helps in visualising the number of zeroes
A polynomial of degree n can have at most n zeroes
Linear, quadratic and cubic polynomials have different graph shapes
examples: A linear polynomial graph is a straight line
A quadratic polynomial graph is a parabola
formulas: Number of zeroes = number of x-axis intersections
exam_tips: Graphical method gives approximate values of zeroes
Degree helps predict number of possible zeroes
Repeated zero means graph touches x-axis
Always relate algebraic zeroes with graphs
